verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचनिशम्यात्मभुवा गीतं कारणं शङ्कयोज्झिता: ।तत: सर्वे न्यवर्तन्त त्रिदिवाय दिवौकस: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaniśamyātma-bhuvā gītaṁkāraṇaṁ śaṅkayojjhitāḥtataḥ sarve nyavartantatridivāya divaukasaḥ
verses.text: Śrī Maitreya said: The demigods, the inhabitants of the higher planets, were freed from all fear upon hearing the cause of the darkness explained by Brahmā, who was born from Viṣṇu. Thus they all returned to their respective planets.

verses.sanskrit: दितिस्तु भर्तुरादेशादपत्यपरिशङ्किनी ।पूर्णेवर्षशते साध्वी पुत्रौ प्रसुषुवे यमौ ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: ditis tu bhartur ādeśādapatya-pariśaṅkinīpūrṇe varṣa-śate sādhvīputrau prasuṣuve yamau
verses.text: The virtuous lady Diti had been very apprehensive of trouble to the gods from the children in her womb, and her husband predicted the same. She brought forth twin sons after a full one hundred years of pregnancy.

verses.sanskrit: उत्पाता बहवस्तत्र निपेतुर्जायमानयो: ।दिविभुव्यन्तरिक्षे च लोकस्योरुभयावहा: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: utpātā bahavas tatranipetur jāyamānayoḥdivi bhuvy antarikṣe calokasyoru-bhayāvahāḥ
verses.text: On the birth of the two demons there were many natural disturbances, all very fearful and wonderful, in the heavenly planets, the earthly planets and in between them.

verses.sanskrit: सहाचला भुवश्चेलुर्दिश: सर्वा:प्रजज्वलु: ।सोल्काश्चाशनय: पेतु: केतवश्चार्तिहेतव: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sahācalā bhuvaś celurdiśaḥ sarvāḥ prajajvaluḥsolkāś cāśanayaḥ petuḥketavaś cārti-hetavaḥ
verses.text: There were earthquakes along the mountains on the earth, and it appeared that there was fire everywhere. Many inauspicious planets like Saturn appeared, along with comets, meteors and thunderbolts.

verses.sanskrit: ववौ वायु: सुदु:स्पर्श: फूत्कारानीरयन्मुहु: ।उन्मूलयन्नगपतीन्वात्यानीको रजोध्वज: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vavau vāyuḥ suduḥsparśaḥphūt-kārān īrayan muhuḥunmūlayan naga-patīnvātyānīko rajo-dhvajaḥ
verses.text: There blew winds which were most uninviting to the touch, hissing again and again and uprooting gigantic trees. They had storms for their armies and clouds of dust for their ensigns.

verses.sanskrit: उद्धसत्तडिदम्भोदघटया नष्टभागणे ।व्योम्नि प्रविष्टतमसा न स्म व्याद‍ृश्यते पदम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: uddhasat-taḍid-ambhoda-ghaṭayā naṣṭa-bhāgaṇevyomni praviṣṭa-tamasāna sma vyādṛśyate padam
verses.text: The luminaries in the heavens were screened by masses of clouds, in which lightning sometimes flashed as though laughing. Darkness reigned everywhere, and nothing could be seen.

verses.sanskrit: चुक्रोश विमना वार्धिरुदूर्मि: क्षुभितोदर: ।सोदपानाश्च सरितश्चुक्षुभु: शुष्कपङ्कजा: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: cukrośa vimanā vārdhirudūrmiḥ kṣubhitodaraḥsodapānāś ca saritaścukṣubhuḥ śuṣka-paṅkajāḥ
verses.text: The ocean with its high waves wailed aloud as if stricken with sorrow, and there was a commotion among the creatures inhabiting the ocean. The rivers and lakes were also agitated, and lotuses withered.

verses.sanskrit: मुहु: परिधयोऽभूवन् सराह्वो: शशिसूर्ययो: ।निर्घाता रथनिर्ह्रादा विवरेभ्य: प्रजज्ञिरे ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: muhuḥ paridhayo ’bhūvansarāhvoḥ śaśi-sūryayoḥnirghātā ratha-nirhrādāvivarebhyaḥ prajajñire
verses.text: Misty halos appeared around the sun and the moon during solar and lunar eclipses again and again. Claps of thunder were heard even without clouds, and sounds like those of rattling chariots emerged from the mountain caves.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तर्ग्रामेषु मुखतो वमन्त्यो वह्निमुल्बणम् ।सृगालोलूकटङ्कारै: प्रणेदुरशिवं शिवा: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: antar-grāmeṣu mukhatovamantyo vahnim ulbaṇamsṛgālolūka-ṭaṅkāraiḥpraṇedur aśivaṁ śivāḥ
verses.text: In the interior of the villages she-jackals yelled portentously, vomiting strong fire from their mouths, and jackals and owls also joined them with their cries.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्गीतवद्रोदनवदुन्नमय्य शिरोधराम् ।व्यमुञ्चन् विविधा वाचो ग्रामसिंहास्ततस्तत: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: saṅgītavad rodanavadunnamayya śirodharāmvyamuñcan vividhā vācogrāma-siṁhās tatas tataḥ
verses.text: Raising their necks, dogs cried here and there, now in the manner of singing and now of wailing.

verses.sanskrit: खराश्च कर्कशै: क्षत्त: खुरैर्घ्नन्तो धरातलम् ।खार्काररभसा मत्ता: पर्यधावन् वरूथश: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: kharāś ca karkaśaiḥ kṣattaḥkhurair ghnanto dharā-talamkhārkāra-rabhasā mattāḥparyadhāvan varūthaśaḥ
verses.text: O Vidura, the asses ran hither and thither in herds, striking the earth with their hard hooves and wildly braying.

verses.sanskrit: रुदन्तो रासभत्रस्ता नीडादुदपतन् खगा: ।घोषेऽरण्ये च पशव: शकृन्मूत्रमकुर्वत ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: rudanto rāsabha-trastānīḍād udapatan khagāḥghoṣe ’raṇye ca paśavaḥśakṛn-mūtram akurvata
verses.text: Frightened by the braying of the asses, birds flew shrieking from their nests, while cattle in the cowsheds as well as in the woods passed dung and urine.

verses.sanskrit: गावोऽत्रसन्नसृग्दोहास्तोयदा: पूयवर्षिण: ।व्यरुदन्देवलिङ्गानि द्रुमा: पेतुर्विनानिलम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: gāvo ’trasann asṛg-dohāstoyadāḥ pūya-varṣiṇaḥvyarudan deva-liṅgānidrumāḥ petur vinānilam
verses.text: Cows, terrified, yielded blood in place of milk, clouds rained pus, the images of the gods in the temples shed tears, and trees fell down without a blast of wind.

verses.sanskrit: ग्रहान् पुण्यतमानन्ये भगणांश्चापि दीपिता: ।अतिचेरुर्वक्रगत्या युयुधुश्च परस्परम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: grahān puṇyatamān anyebhagaṇāṁś cāpi dīpitāḥaticerur vakra-gatyāyuyudhuś ca parasparam
verses.text: Ominous planets such as Mars and Saturn shone brighter and surpassed the auspicious ones such as Mercury, Jupiter and Venus as well as a number of lunar mansions. Taking seemingly retrograde courses, the planets came in conflict with one another.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वान्यांश्च महोत्पातानतत्तत्त्वविद: प्रजा: ।ब्रह्मपुत्रानृते भीता मेनिरे विश्‍वसम्प्लवम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvānyāṁś ca mahotpātānatat-tattva-vidaḥ prajāḥbrahma-putrān ṛte bhītāmenire viśva-samplavam
verses.text: Marking these and many other omens of evil times, everyone but the four sage sons of Brahmā, who were aware of the fall of Jaya and Vijaya and of their birth as Diti’s sons, was seized with fear. They did not know the secrets of these portents and thought that the dissolution of the universe was at hand.

verses.sanskrit: तावादिदैत्यौ सहसा व्यज्यमानात्मपौरुषौ ।ववृधातेऽश्मसारेण कायेनाद्रिपती इव ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāv ādi-daityau sahasāvyajyamānātma-pauruṣauvavṛdhāte ’śma-sāreṇakāyenādri-patī iva
verses.text: These two demons who appeared in ancient times soon began to exhibit uncommon bodily features; they had steel-like frames which began to grow just like two great mountains.

verses.sanskrit: दिविस्पृशौ हेमकिरीटकोटिभि-र्निरुद्धकाष्ठौ स्फुरदङ्गदाभुजौ ।गां कम्पयन्तौ चरणै: पदे पदेकट्या सुकाञ्‍च्यार्कमतीत्य तस्थतु: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: divi-spṛśau hema-kirīṭa-koṭibhirniruddha-kāṣṭhau sphurad-aṅgadā-bhujaugāṁ kampayantau caraṇaiḥ pade padekaṭyā sukāñcyārkam atītya tasthatuḥ
verses.text: Their bodies became so tall that they seemed to kiss the sky with the crests of their gold crowns. They blocked the view of all directions and while walking shook the earth at every step. Their arms were adorned with brilliant bracelets, and they stood as if covering the sun with their waists, which were bound with excellent and beautiful girdles.

verses.sanskrit: प्रजापतिर्नाम तयोरकार्षीद्य: प्राक् स्वदेहाद्यमयोरजायत ।तं वै हिरण्यकशिपुं विदु: प्रजायं तं हिरण्याक्षमसूत साग्रत: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: prajāpatir nāma tayor akārṣīdyaḥ prāk sva-dehād yamayor ajāyatataṁ vai hiraṇyakaśipuṁ viduḥ prajāyaṁ taṁ hiraṇyākṣam asūta sāgrataḥ
verses.text: Prajāpati Kaśyapa, the creator of the living entities, gave his twin sons their names; the one who was born first he named Hiraṇyākṣa, and the one who was first conceived by Diti he named Hiraṇyakaśipu.

verses.sanskrit: चक्रे हिरण्यकशिपुर्दोर्भ्यां ब्रह्मवरेण च ।वशे सपालाँल्लोकांस्त्रीनकुतोमृत्युरुद्धत: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: cakre hiraṇyakaśipurdorbhyāṁ brahma-vareṇa cavaśe sa-pālāḻ lokāṁs trīnakuto-mṛtyur uddhataḥ
verses.text: The elder child, Hiraṇyakaśipu, was unafraid of death from anyone within the three worlds because he received a benediction from Lord Brahmā. He was proud and puffed up due to this benediction and was able to bring all three planetary systems under his control.

verses.sanskrit: हिरण्याक्षोऽनुजस्तस्य प्रिय: प्रीतिकृदन्वहम् ।गदापाणिर्दिवं यातो युयुत्सुर्मृगयन् रणम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: hiraṇyākṣo ’nujas tasyapriyaḥ prīti-kṛd anvahamgadā-pāṇir divaṁ yātoyuyutsur mṛgayan raṇam
verses.text: His younger brother, Hiraṇyākṣa, was always ready to satisfy his elder brother by his activities. Hiraṇyākṣa took a club on his shoulder and traveled all over the universe with a fighting spirit just to satisfy Hiraṇyakaśipu.

verses.sanskrit: तं वीक्ष्य दु:सहजवं रणत्काञ्चननूपुरम् ।वैजयन्त्या स्रजा जुष्टमंसन्यस्तमहागदम् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vīkṣya duḥsaha-javaṁraṇat-kāñcana-nūpuramvaijayantyā srajā juṣṭamaṁsa-nyasta-mahā-gadam
verses.text: Hiraṇyākṣa’s temper was difficult to control. He had anklets of gold tinkling about his feet, he was adorned with a gigantic garland, and he rested his huge mace on one of his shoulders.

verses.sanskrit: मनोवीर्यवरोत्सिक्तमसृण्यमकुतोभयम् ।भीता निलिल्यिरे देवास्तार्क्ष्यत्रस्ता इवाहय: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: mano-vīrya-varotsiktamasṛṇyam akuto-bhayambhītā nililyire devāstārkṣya-trastā ivāhayaḥ
verses.text: His mental and bodily strength as well as the boon conferred upon him had made him proud. He feared death at the hands of no one, and there was no checking him. The gods, therefore, were seized with fear at his very sight, and they hid themselves even as snakes hide themselves for fear of Garuḍa.

verses.sanskrit: स वै तिरोहितान् दृष्ट्वा महसा स्वेन दैत्यराट् ।सेन्द्रान्देवगणान् क्षीबानपश्यन् व्यनदद् भृशम् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vai tirohitān dṛṣṭvāmahasā svena daitya-rāṭsendrān deva-gaṇān kṣībānapaśyan vyanadad bhṛśam
verses.text: On not finding Indra and the other demigods, who had previously been intoxicated with power, the chief of the Daityas, seeing that they had all vanished before his might, roared loudly.

verses.sanskrit: ततो निवृत्त: क्रीडिष्यन् गम्भीरं भीमनिस्वनम् ।विजगाहे महासत्त्वो वार्धिं मत्त इव द्विप: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato nivṛttaḥ krīḍiṣyangambhīraṁ bhīma-nisvanamvijagāhe mahā-sattvovārdhiṁ matta iva dvipaḥ
verses.text: After returning from the heavenly kingdom, the mighty demon, who was like an elephant in wrath, for the sake of sport dived into the deep ocean, which was roaring terribly.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् प्रविष्टे वरुणस्य सैनिकायादोगणा: सन्नधिय: ससाध्वसा: ।अहन्यमाना अपि तस्य वर्चसाप्रधर्षिता दूरतरं प्रदुद्रुवु: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmin praviṣṭe varuṇasya sainikāyādo-gaṇāḥ sanna-dhiyaḥ sasādhvasāḥahanyamānā api tasya varcasāpradharṣitā dūrataraṁ pradudruvuḥ
verses.text: On his entering the ocean, the aquatic animals who formed the host of Varuṇa were stricken with fear and ran far away. Thus Hiraṇyākṣa showed his splendor without dealing a blow.

verses.sanskrit: स वर्षपूगानुदधौ महाबल-श्चरन्महोर्मीञ्‍छ्वसनेरितान्मुहु: ।मौर्व्याभिजघ्ने गदया विभावरी-मासेदिवांस्तात पुरीं प्रचेतस: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa varṣa-pūgān udadhau mahā-balaścaran mahormīñ chvasaneritān muhuḥmaurvyābhijaghne gadayā vibhāvarīmāsedivāṁs tāta purīṁ pracetasaḥ
verses.text: Moving about in the ocean for many, many years, the mighty Hiraṇyākṣa smote the gigantic wind-tossed waves again and again with his iron mace and reached Vibhāvarī, the capital of Varuṇa.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोपलभ्यासुरलोकपालकंयादोगणानामृषभं प्रचेतसम् ।स्मयन् प्रलब्धुं प्रणिपत्य नीचव-ज्जगाद मे देह्यधिराज संयुगम ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatropalabhyāsura-loka-pālakaṁyādo-gaṇānām ṛṣabhaṁ pracetasamsmayan pralabdhuṁ praṇipatya nīcavajjagāda me dehy adhirāja saṁyugam
verses.text: Vibhāvarī is the home of Varuṇa, lord of the aquatic creatures and guardian of the lower regions of the universe, where the demons generally reside. There Hiraṇyākṣa fell at Varuṇa’s feet like a lowborn man, and to make fun of him he said with a smile, “Give me battle, O Supreme Lord!”

verses.sanskrit: त्वं लोकपालोऽधिपतिर्बृहच्छ्रवावीर्यापहो दुर्मदवीरमानिनाम् ।विजित्य लोकेऽखिलदैत्यदानवान्यद्राजसूयेन पुरायजत्प्रभो ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ loka-pālo ’dhipatir bṛhac-chravāvīryāpaho durmada-vīra-mānināmvijitya loke ’khila-daitya-dānavānyad rājasūyena purāyajat prabho
verses.text: You are the guardian of an entire sphere and a ruler of wide fame. Having crushed the might of arrogant and conceited warriors and having conquered all the Daityas and Dānavas in the world, you once performed a Rājasūya sacrifice to the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: स एवमुत्सिक्तमदेन विद्विषाद‍ृढं प्रलब्धो भगवानपां पति: ।रोषं समुत्थं शमयन् स्वया धियाव्यवोचदङ्गोपशमं गता वयम् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa evam utsikta-madena vidviṣādṛḍhaṁ pralabdho bhagavān apāṁ patiḥroṣaṁ samutthaṁ śamayan svayā dhiyāvyavocad aṅgopaśamaṁ gatā vayam
verses.text: Thus mocked by an enemy whose vanity knew no bounds, the worshipful lord of the waters waxed angry, but by dint of his reason he managed to curb the anger that had sprung up in him, and he replied: O dear one, we have now desisted from warfare, having grown too old for combat.

verses.sanskrit: पश्यामि नान्यं पुरुषात्पुरातनाद्य: संयुगे त्वां रणमार्गकोविदम् ।आराधयिष्यत्यसुरर्षभेहि तंमनस्विनो यं गृणते भवाद‍ृशा: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: paśyāmi nānyaṁ puruṣāt purātanādyaḥ saṁyuge tvāṁ raṇa-mārga-kovidamārādhayiṣyaty asurarṣabhehi taṁmanasvino yaṁ gṛṇate bhavādṛśāḥ
verses.text: You are so skilled in war that I do not see anyone else but the most ancient person, Lord Viṣṇu, who can give satisfaction in battle to you. Therefore, O chief of the asuras, approach Him, whom even heroes like you mention with praise.

verses.sanskrit: तं वीरमारादभिपद्य विस्मय:शयिष्यसे वीरशये श्वभिर्वृत: ।यस्त्वद्विधानामसतां प्रशान्तयेरूपाणि धत्ते सदनुग्रहेच्छया ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vīram ārād abhipadya vismayaḥśayiṣyase vīra-śaye śvabhir vṛtaḥyas tvad-vidhānām asatāṁ praśāntayerūpāṇi dhatte sad-anugrahecchayā
verses.text: Varuṇa continued: On reaching Him you will be rid of your pride at once and will lie down on the field of battle, surrounded by dogs, for eternal sleep. It is in order to exterminate wicked fellows like you and to show His grace to the virtuous that He assumes His various incarnations like Varāha.
